meaning: n.盲人用点字法vt.用盲字印
examples: How many official documents have been transcribed into Braille for blind people?
Unfortunately, fewer than 5 percent of books are now available in Braille or audiotape.
My fingers scan a book in braille.
Braille displays connected to computers largely solve this literacy issue but are sometimes prohibitively expensive.
His first company was a Braille publishing enterprise which served an international client base.
